item.description: A case was filed against a journalist in Rajshahi on allegations of sexually harassing a female colleague......

item.title: Appointment of city corporation administrators challenged in HC
item.description: A writ petition was filed with the High Court on Wednesday challenging the appointment of administrators to six city corporations, including Dhaka North City Corporation and Dhaka South City Corporation......

item.title: Gono Bishwabidyalay rape victim alleges intimidation
item.description: A female student of privately owned Gono Bishwabidyalay at Savar, who is a complainant in a 2025 rape case, has alleged intimidation by associates of one of the accused, including the vice-president of the university’s central students’ union......Foreign affairs analysis

item.title: EC publishes centre-wise results of 13th nat’l polls
item.description: The election commission has published the centre-wise results of the 13th national parliament elections......

item.title: Nahid urges NCP to be prepared for local polls
item.description: Opposition chief whip and convener of the National Citizen Party Nahid Islam on Wednesday called on party leaders and activists to prepare for the forthcoming local government elections, saying they must get ready for another fight and enter the field this time with full strength and no mistakes......

item.title: SME Foundation, Save the Children join hands to promote youth entrepreneurship
item.description: SME Foundation and Save the Children in Bangladesh signed an agreement on Wednesday to implement programmes focused on youth entrepreneurship development jointly......

item.title: Minister suggests UGC to formulate integrated plan for higher education dev
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon has suggested the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh to improve the quality of higher education by formulating integrated plans on its own initiative, not limiting the activities of the country’s universities to the level of supervision alone......

item.title: BUFT hosts orientation for new students
item.description: BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology organised an orientation programme to welcome newly admitted students of the spring semester 2026 on its permanent campus at Nishatnagar of Turag in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Allen's record ton powers New Zealand into T20 World Cup final
item.description:
New Zealand's Finn Allen blasted the fastest century in T20 World Cup history in Kolkata on Wednesday as his side hammered South Africa by nine wickets to reach the final.
...

item.title: RMU VC’s removal demanded over graft allegations
item.description: A group of people on Wednesday formed a human chain in Rajshahi city demanding removal of Rajshahi Medical University vice-chancellor, following allegations that he demanded a commission from contractors in exchange for awarding development work......

item.title: Disaster management dept asst engineer, wife’s tax docs seized
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday ordered the seizure of tax documents of Md Anwarul Islam, an assistant engineer of the Mujib Kella project under the department of disaster management, and his wife Tahmina Akter......

item.title: DIG of APBN calls on CHT minister
item.description: DIG (APBN) Salma Begum paid a courtesy call on Chittagong Hill Tracts affairs minister Dipen Dewan at his Rangamati residence on Tuesday......

item.title: BMU forms body of pharmacists, technologists
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University has formed a 25-member Medical Technologist and Pharmacist Association on Tuesday, according to an order signed by BMU proctor Sheikh Farhad......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: ICT orders newspaper notices for Shamim Osman, 11 others
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Wednesday ordered the publication of newspaper notices asking former Awami League lawmaker AKM Shamim Osman and 11 others to appear before it in a crimes against humanity case over killings in Narayanganj during the 2024 July uprising......

item.title: Govt preparing lists of extortionists, armed criminals: home minister
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Wednesday said that the government was preparing lists of extortionists and armed criminals across the country to bring them to book......

item.title: 10 killed, 476 injured in post‑polls violence: report
item.description: Ten people, including a child, were killed and 476 injured in 104 incidents of post‑election violence in the country, reported rights organisation Odhikar in a publication that was launched on Wednesday......

item.title: 25 more ME flights cancelled
item.description: At least 25 more Middle Eastern flights have been cancelled to and from Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka city on Wednesday, raising the total number of flight cancellation to 173 in the past five days since Saturday......

item.title: Travel ban on Lotus Kamal’s partner, 2 Eastern Ins officials
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday imposed travel ban on Mohammad Abu Noman, a business partner of former finance minister AHM Mustafa Kamal, popularly known as Lotus Kamal, his daughter Nafisa Kamal, former deputy managing director of Eastern Insurance Company Limited Mahmud Hasan and its former senior executive vice-president Jannatun Nesa Jakia over graft allegations......

item.title: PM to launch Family Card March 10, Farmer Card April 14
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman will formally inaugurate the Family Card programme at 10:00am on March 10 at the T&T ground adjacent to Korail slum in Dhaka......

item.title: ICT defers verdict
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Wednesday deferred the verdict in a crimes against humanity case against former Dhaka Metropolitan Police commissioner Habibur Rahman and four other officers over killings and injuries in the capital’s Rampura area during the 2024 July uprising......

item.title: 87 killed as US sinks Iranian warship
item.description: A US submarine sank an Iranian warship off the coast of Sri Lanka on Wednesday, killing at least 87 sailors and leaving dozens missing, officials said......

item.title: Govt plans women-only buses despite driver, capacity crisis
item.description: The government has initiated a move to introduce a women-only bus service in the capital despite limited institutional capacity and the lacklustre performance of existing similar services......

item.title: Quantum computing and future of digital security
item.description: SECURITY has never been absolute. It exists only in relation to the strength and capability of a potential attacker. A simple analogy illustrates this. A person walking alone at night faces a certain level of risk. If the attacker carries a knife, awareness and physical response may still matter. If the attacker carries a firearm, the balance of power shifts immediately. Individual......

item.title: Political transition and strategic balance
item.description: THE outcome of Bangladesh’s 13th parliamentary election marks a decisive political transition following the ouster of Sheikh Hasina during the mass uprising of 2024 and the subsequent collapse of Awami League rule. The Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s victory has ended over a decade of centralised governance and introduced a new political leadership under......

item.title: Preparedness reduces earthquake losses
item.description: WHEN the earth shakes, it does not ask whether a city is ready. It exposes the truth in seconds. Prime minister Tarique Rahman’s recent directive to prepare one hundred thousand volunteers in Dhaka for post-earthquake response is, at first glance, an administrative instruction. Look closer and it is something more serious: an acknowledgment that Bangladesh’s seismic risk is no longer theoretical. It is immediate, measurable and unforgiving......

item.title: Greening the textile dyeing and printing industry
item.description: THE textile and garment industry is a crucial part of Bangladesh’s economy, playing a major role in exports, employment and industrial growth. However, this industry faces significant environmental challenges, especially in dyeing and printing, which are among the most resource-consuming and polluting stages of textile manufacturing. These facilities often use large......

item.title: Constitutional legitimacy or political strategy?
item.description: BANGLADESH has recently has passed through a turbulent, transformative phase. In that context, the interim government, headed by Muhammad Yunus, assumed office amid a political reality in which an effective parliament was absent, demands for administrative restructuring were strong and expectations for reforms in state institutions were intense. In such......

item.title: Govt should act to save cane farming, sugar mills
item.description: THIS is a matter of concern that whilst the closure of six of the 15 state-owned sugar mills and some expense reduction steps have failed to make the remaining ventures profitable, farmers appear gradually to be losing interest in sugarcane farming. All the while, five privately-owned refineries keep dominating the domestic sugar market. Sugarcane farming in the......

item.title: Govt must end cycle of wage theft
item.description: LABOUR unrest before Eid has become commonplace as factory owners rarely pay festival allowances in time. This year is also no different. A labour ministry report, based on police findings, shows that around 1.38 lakh workers in 180 apparel factories are likely to hold protests because wages and festival allowances might not be paid in time. The report categorises the......

item.title: Publishers, readers hopeful
item.description: After the first week of the Amar Ekushey Book Fair, publishers, organisers and readers are still hopeful about the success of the 18-day book fair......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Wife of abducted trader urges police to rescue him alive
item.description: Reshma Khatun, wife of Jahangir Alam, 48, owner of RR Medical and JR Agrovate at Shahkarpur in Jashore town, at a press conference urged the law enforcement agencies to do their best to rescue her husband alive......

item.title: Four arrested for shooting narcotics inspector
item.description: The police have arrested four individuals and recovered three firearms in connection with shooting and injuring an official of the Department of Narcotics Control during a recent anti-drug drive in the capital’s Sayedabad area......

item.title: Govt urged to announce holiday on March 18 for Eid-time travel
item.description: Passenger Welfare Association of Bangladesh on Wednesday called on the government to extend the one-day holiday on March 18 by executive order to facilitate the Eid-ul-Fitr travellers to ensure comfortable journey......

item.title: Protests continue against attack on Iran
item.description: Differentpoliticalparties and organisations on Wednesday continued holding rallies and processions in Dhaka protesting against the attacks on Iran by the military forces of the United States and Israel......Top news summaries

item.title: India keen to work closely with new govt: envoy
item.description: Indian high commissioner to Dhaka Pranay Verma on Wednesday said that his country was keen to work closely with the new government of Bangladesh......

item.title: ACC sues ex-chairman, MD of Fareast life scam
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission filed case against six persons, including former chairman and managing director of Fareast Islami Life Insurance Company Limited, for embezzling over Tk 13.71 crore through making fake work order and bill-voucher on civil and electro-mechanical work of the Fareast Tower-2......

item.title: Canal excavation to begin in mid-March
item.description: The government is set to inaugurate a countrywide canal excavation and restoration programme in mid-March, ahead of Eid-ul-Fitr......

item.title: Nepal readies for pivotal post-uprising vote
item.description: Nepal set up polling centres on Wednesday for one of its most hotly contested elections since the end of civil war in 2006, six months after deadly anti-corruption protests toppled the government......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Gulf defences unprepared for Iranian drones, analysts say
item.description: Wwup long-range drone strikes against Gulf countries, Iran is exploiting the inadequacy of their defences against the low-cost threat in a bid to pressure the United States to end the war, analysts said......
